title: API key management: create, list, revoke, and rotate

story.i_want_to: create additional API keys with specific roles, list existing keys (showing prefix and metadata only), revoke keys, and rotate keys (generating a new key with a grace period on the old one)
story.so_that: I can provision separate API keys for different agents and orchestrators, manage their lifecycle, and rotate credentials without downtime

rationale: A tenant needs multiple API keys: one for each agent, one for the orchestrator, and user-level keys for administration. Key rotation is critical for security hygiene — the rotate endpoint creates a new key and keeps the old one valid for a grace period so agents can transition without downtime. Listing keys must never expose the raw key or hash, only the prefix.

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /api/v1/api_keys (role: user) creates a new API key with specified name and role; returns the raw key once in the response
The create endpoint accepts: name (required), role (required, one of: user, orchestrator, agent), expires_at (optional), agent_id (optional, for agent-role keys)
Users cannot create superadmin keys; attempting to do so returns 403
GET /api/v1/api_keys (role: user) lists all API keys for the current tenant, showing: id, name, key_prefix, role, last_used_at, expires_at, revoked_at, inserted_at. Never exposes key_hash or raw key.
DELETE /api/v1/api_keys/:id (role: user) revokes an API key by setting revoked_at to the current time; does not delete the database record
POST /api/v1/api_keys/:id/rotate (role: user) creates a new key with the same name/role and sets expires_at on the old key to now + grace_period (default 24 hours, configurable in request body)
The rotate response includes both the new raw key and the old key's updated expiration time
All endpoints are scoped to the current tenant — a user cannot see or manage keys from another tenant
API key count per tenant is limited by the max_api_keys tenant setting (default 100); creating beyond the limit returns 422
Every mutation (create, update, delete) MUST be recorded in the audit log via Ecto.Multi in the same transaction as the entity change.

test_cases.name: Create API key with valid attributes
steps: POST /api/v1/api_keys with Authorization: Bearer <raw_key> and body {"name": "agent-1", "role": "agent"}
expected_results: Response status is 201
Response body has api_key.raw_key starting with 'lc_'
Response body has api_key.key_prefix, api_key.role = "agent"
New key exists in database with correct tenant_id

test_cases.name: List API keys shows prefix only
expected_results: Response status is 200
Response body has a list of API keys
Each key has key_prefix but no key_hash or raw_key field
Both keys for this tenant are listed

test_cases.name: Revoke API key sets revoked_at
expected_results: Response status is 200
The agent API key now has revoked_at set
The agent raw key can no longer authenticate

test_cases.name: Rotate API key creates new key and sets grace period
preconditions: fixture(:api_key, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, role: :agent, name: "worker"}) returns {_, old_key}
steps: POST /api/v1/api_keys/<old_key.id>/rotate with Authorization: Bearer <admin_key> and body {"grace_period_hours": 12}
expected_results: Response status is 201
Response body has new_key.raw_key (different from old key)
Response body has old_key_expires_at approximately 12 hours from now
The old key still works during the grace period
The new key works immediately
New key has the same name and role as the old key

test_cases.name: Cannot create superadmin key via tenant endpoint
expected_results: Response status is 403
No superadmin key is created

test_cases.name: Cannot see another tenant's keys
expected_results: Only keys belonging to tenant_a are returned
Tenant_b's keys are not visible

test_cases.name: Key creation respects max_api_keys limit
expected_results: Response status is 422
Error message indicates API key limit reached

test_cases.name: Mutation creates audit log entries for API key management operations
steps: POST /api/v1/api_keys with user API key and body {"name": "new-agent", "role": "agent"}
DELETE /api/v1/api_keys/:target_key_id with user API key to revoke it
expected_results: Audit log entry created with entity_type='api_key', entity_id=new_key.id, action='created', actor_type='api_key', actor_id=api_key.id
Audit log entry created with entity_type='api_key', entity_id=target_key.id, action='revoked', actor_type='api_key', actor_id=api_key.id
Audit log entry created with entity_type='api_key', entity_id=rotated_key.id, action='rotated', actor_type='api_key', actor_id=api_key.id
All audit entries are in the same transaction as the entity changes (no orphaned entity without audit)

technical_notes: The create endpoint must use the JSON view that includes raw_key — same pattern as registration
Rotation is essentially: create new key + update old key's expires_at in a transaction
The list endpoint should exclude revoked keys by default, with an optional ?include_revoked=true query param
Tenant scoping is enforced both by application (WHERE tenant_id) and RLS
Consider adding pagination to the list endpoint (offset/limit) for tenants with many keys
All context functions MUST return `{:ok, result}` or `{:error, reason}` tuples. Never raise exceptions for expected error cases.
Controller MUST use `action_fallback LoopctlWeb.FallbackController` for error rendering (maps error atoms to HTTP status codes per US-1.4)
All test modules MUST use `async: true` (e.g., `use Loopctl.DataCase, async: true` or `use LoopctlWeb.ConnCase, async: true`)
All tests using Mox MUST include `setup :verify_on_exit!`
All mocks MUST be defined centrally in `test/support/mocks.ex`, never in individual test files
